MATHEMATICS QUIZ — Quadratic Equations
Class: SSS3 | Board: WAEC/NERDC | Time: 30 minutes

Q1. [ANALYZE] [Bloom: ANALYZE] [Marks: 4]
Two students, Ngozi and Emeka, each solve the equation x² − 5x + 6 = 0.
Ngozi gets x = 2 and x = 3. Emeka gets x = −2 and x = −3.
Analyse both solutions and determine which is correct. Show your working using the factorisation method and compare the two results.

Q2. [MULTIPLE_CHOICE] [Bloom: ANALYZE] [Marks: 2]
The discriminant of a quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0 is b² − 4ac = 0. Analyse what this tells us about the roots:
A) The equation has two distinct real roots
B) The equation has no real roots
C) The equation has one repeated real root
D) The equation has two complex roots
Answer: C

Q3. [SHORT_ANSWER] [Bloom: ANALYZE] [Marks: 4]
Examine the following three quadratic equations and distinguish which method (factorisation, completing the square, or quadratic formula) is most efficient for each. Justify your choice:
   a) x² − 9 = 0
   b) x² + 6x + 1 = 0
   c) 2x² − 5x − 3 = 0

Q4. [ANALYZE] [Bloom: ANALYZE] [Marks: 4]
A rectangular farm in Benue State has an area of 60 m². Its length is 4 m more than its width. Tunde writes the equation: w(w + 4) = 60.
   a) Expand and rearrange this into the standard form ax² + bx + c = 0.
   b) Analyse whether this quadratic has real solutions using the discriminant.
   c) Solve for the width and contrast the two roots — which is physically meaningful and why?

Q5. [SHORT_ANSWER] [Bloom: ANALYZE] [Marks: 3]
Examine the graph of y = x² − 4x + 3.
   a) Compare the value of x at the points where the graph crosses the x-axis with the roots of x² − 4x + 3 = 0.
   b) Distinguish between the roots and the vertex of the parabola.
reference: NERDC/WAEC SSS3 Mathematics quiz on quadratic equations requiring analysis of solutions, discriminant interpretation, and method comparison using Nigerian contexts.
subject: mathematics
